司令官、教会に用事ですか？: Commander, do you have business at the church?
少しマーガレットの様子が気になってな。: I was just curious about how Margaret's doing.
あ、なるほど。神の教えを広めたいとおっしゃっていましたけど<br>うまくいってい:
Ah, I see. She said she wanted to spread God's teachings, but I
wonder if it's going well...
――教会の祭壇の下に大勢の人が集まっていた。<br>その中心にはマーガレットが立:
There was a large crowd gathered beneath the church altar. At the
center stood Margaret.
はっはっは。マーガレットさん！ 前歯に隙間があって<br>いつも気になってたんで:
Ha ha ha! Margaret! I always used to be self-conscious about the gap
in my front teeth, but now it doesn't bother me at all!
ふふ、それは良かったです。<br>心健やかにお過ごしください。: Fufu, that's wonderful. I hope you remain at peace.
うちも、息子がいっつもドアを開けっぱなしにするの<br>どーでもよくなっちゃいま:
And for me too! My son always leaves the door open, but now I don't
even care about that anymore!
奥さんが細かいこと気にしなくなったんで、俺も幸せでーす！<br>マーガレットさん:
My wife doesn't worry about the little things anymore, so I'm a happy
man! Margaret, you're the best!
私は何もしていません。<br>少し楽になるよう、お声掛けしただけですから。:
I haven't done anything. I only spoke to them to help ease their
burdens.
私はただきっかけを与えただけ――<br>皆さんの気持ちを明るくしているのは、皆さ:
I only gave them a push. It is they themselves who brighten their own
hearts. Fufu.
マーガレット様。これから出撃なので<br>無事を祈っていただけないでしょうか！:
Lady Margaret. We're about to head out on a sortie. Could you pray
for our safety?
私もお願いします！<br>私たち、戻ったら式を挙げる予定でして――: Me too, please! We're planning to get married when we return...
分かりました。力の限りお祈りいたします。<br>神のご加護がありますように――:
Of course. I will pray with all my might. May God's blessings be upon
you.
ふぅ――司令官様、アリシアさん。<br>お待たせしてしまって、大変申し訳ございま:
*sigh*... Lord Commander, Alicia. I deeply apologize for keeping you
waiting.
問題ない。<br>そっちは、ずいぶん調子が良さそうじゃないか。: No problem. Things look pretty good on your end, don't they?
おかげさまで。<br>皆さんの表情が日々、変わっていくのがとても嬉しいです。:
Thanks to you. I'm so happy to see everyone's expressions changing
day by day.
司令官様やアリシアさんと近しい方も<br>教会に足を運んでくださっています。:
People close to you, Lord Commander, and Alicia have also been
making their way to the church.
皆さん、それぞれご自身の行動で思うところがあったようなのですが<br>改善の兆し:
It seems each of them had reflected on their actions, and there are
signs of improvement. That truly fills me with joy.
ほう？<br>……ちなみに、誰のことなんだ？: Huh? ...By the way, who are you talking about?
複数名おられるのですが――ソフィアさんは、真面目過ぎるところを直したいと<br>:
There are several, actually. It seems Sophia wanted to fix her overly
serious nature, so I suggested she relax a little.
（……そういえば、この間すれ違ったとき、<br>大あくびを見られたのに何も言われ:
(...Come to think of it, when we passed by the other day, I let out a
huge yawn, and she didn't say anything. I thought it was strange.)
ベリサさんは興味本位で来てくれた際に「皆さんに敬意を持ってみては？」と<br>提:
Verisa came out of curiosity. I suggested she show everyone more
respect, and she is happily putting it into practice.
以前から皆さんには表面上、丁寧な受け答えをする方でしたけど、<br>今はそれが自:
Before, she was always polite on the surface, but now she tells me
she is glad that it comes naturally in conversation.
マリナさんは、お金に対する執着が少し弱まったようです。: Marina's attachment to money seems to have weakened a bit.
それはもう洗脳だろう！？<br>どうやった！？: That's brainwashing, isn't it! How did you do it?
ただ、普通に神の教えを説いただけですよ。: I simply preached God's teachings, that is all.
ヒマリさんには積極的になれたことを、クルルさんには落ち着いて行動が<br>できる:
Himari was happy that she could become more proactive, and Kururu
was glad that she could act more calmly. Ufufu.
……アリシア。<br>俺は今、すべての人間の中でマーガレットが一番怖い。: ...Alicia. Right now, out of all people, I'm most afraid of Margaret.
ま、まぁ……でも、皆さん喜んでいるならいいのでは？<br>強制もしていないようで:
W-Well... but if everyone's happy, isn't that fine? It doesn't seem like
she's forcing anything, either...
皆さん、自分から悩みを打ち明けたり、自分で考えた上で、<br>マーガレットさんの:
Everyone comes to her voluntarily with their troubles. They think
things through and put her advice into practice, right?
はい。私からは押し付けません。<br>それは、神の教えに反しますから。: Yes. I do not force them. That would go against God's teachings.
皆さんが抱く「自分の気になるところ」も否定はしません。<br>それも、神が与えた:
I also do not deny the things each person is concerned about. Those,
too, are gifts from God that belong to that person.
変化の有無は重要ではなく、大事なのは、自分の在り方を見つめ直し、<br>努力を続:
Whether change occurs is unimportant. What matters is reexamining
one's own being and striving—that is the nobility God seeks in a person.
なるほど……！ やっぱりマーガレットさんのお話は素晴らしいです！<br>聞いてい:
I see... As expected, your words are wonderful, Margaret! Listening
to you, I learn so much!
あ～……感激しているところ悪いが、俺はそろそろ行くぞ。<br>仕事がまだ残ってい:
Ahh... sorry to break the mood, but I'm heading out now. I've still got
work to do.
いずれ、司令官様のお話も聞かせてくださいね。<br>いつでもお待ちしています。:
One day, please let me hear your story too, Lord Commander. I am
always waiting.
はぁっ……マーガレットさんのお話、本当に素敵でした。<br>心が洗われましたよ。:
*sigh*... Margaret's story was truly wonderful. It felt like my soul was
cleansed! I want to become someone like that, too.
……そうか。<br>俺にはよく分からん感覚だ。: I see... That's a feeling I can't really relate to.
ああいうタイプは苦手ですか？: Not a fan of that type, Commander?
分からないでもないですけどね。<br>マーガレットさん、司令官とは正反対のタイプ:
I can kind of understand though. After all, Margaret is the polar
opposite of you, Commander...
マーガレットの人となりを否定するつもりはないし、<br>価値も認めてはいるが、そ:
I'm not denying who Margaret is, and I recognize her value, but you
can't get through life on that alone.
善人であるからこそ、そろそろ面倒事が起きるころだ。<br>大事にならなければいい:
It's precisely because she's such a good person that trouble's bound
to find her soon. I just hope it doesn't blow up...
司令官！ 大変です！<br>マーガレットさんが盗賊団に誘拐されました！:
Commander, this is serious! Margaret has been kidnapped by a bandit
gang.
教会で普段通りに皆さんと過ごしていたところに、<br>怪しい男が救いを求めて押し:
They say a suspicious man showed up at the church seeking salvation
while she was with everyone as usual, and—
マーガレットさんは言われた通りにアジトへ説法に向かったのですが<br>その後、身:
Margaret went to his hideout to give a sermon as asked, but later, a
ransom letter was delivered to you, Commander.
……なかなかの大金だな。しかも、取引場所は大穴内部。<br>それも、俺１人で来る:
...That's quite a sum. And the meeting place is inside the Abyss. They
want me to come alone, huh.
仕方ない。すぐに向かうぞ。: Can't be helped. I'll head out right now.
そんな！？<br>マーガレットさんは心配ですが、さすがに危険ですよ！？:
Are you serious? I'm worried about Margaret, but that's way too
dangerous!
大丈夫だ。<br>おそらくマーガレットの場合――: It's fine. In Margaret's case, probably—
さて、約束の場所に来たぞ。<br>マーガレットと盗賊たちは――あそこか。:
Alright, I've arrived at the meeting spot. Margaret and the bandits
are—over there, huh.
おーいおいおいおい！ なんでだぁ！ なんで、あんたの話を聞いてたら<br>涙が止:
Oi, oi, oi, oi! Why can't I stop crying when I listen to your story, I tell
ya.
そうさせているのは私ではないんです。<br>涙を流しているのは、あなたの心なんで: I'm not the one making you do that. Your own heart is crying.
窃盗は罪です。それは間違いありませんが、<br>取り返しがつかない行為でもない―:
Stealing is a sin. There's no doubt about that, but it's not an
irredeemable act either.
罪を悔いて、これから善行を重ねる――それさえできれば<br>悪行も善行のために必:
If you repent of your sins and accumulate good deeds from now on,
even your past misdeeds become part of the path that led you to virtue.
そういった道を望む皆さんの心が、涙を流させているんです。<br>今からでも変われ:
Your hearts, yearning for that path, are what bring you to tears. Can
you change, even starting now?
そうさなぁ……そうだよなぁ……ううっ<br>このままじゃ故郷のかーちゃんに顔向け:
That's right... yeah, you're right... Uuh... At this rate, I can't face my
ma back home!
取り込んでいるところすまない。<br>金を持ってきたんだが、受け取ってくれるか？: Sorry to interrupt. I brought the money, but... will you take it?
あ！ 前線基地の司令官でヤンス！？: Ah! That's the Commander of the Frontline Base, ain't it?
……わざわざ来てもらったんだが、<br>もう悪いことして稼いだ金なんていらねぇぞ:
...You took the trouble to come, but we don't want no money earned
by doing bad stuff anymore!
そうでヤンス！ 金を受け取ったら、司令官の評判を落とすために<br>マーガレット:
That's right! We was gonna take the money and kill Margaret to ruin
the Commander's reputation, but we ain't doin' that no more.
そうだそうだ！ これからはまっとうな道で生きる！:
Yeah, that's right. From now on, we're gonna live the straight and
narrow!
いや、できる！ できるまでやればいいってだけよぉ！<br>やるぞぉぉおオオオー！: No, we can. We just gotta keep at it until we do. Let's do this!
（話をしただけなのに、自分が助かるどころか、盗賊たちを改心させた――？<br>そ:
(She just talks to them, and instead of getting herself rescued, she
reforms the bandits? Is that even possible?)
司令官様。ご心配をお掛けして申し訳ございません。<br>来てくださったこと、感謝:
Lord Commander. I apologize for worrying you. I am grateful that you
came for me.
あぁ、いや――実を言うと、心配はあまりしてなかった。<br>こんなことになるんじ:
Ah, well... to be honest, I wasn't that worried. I had a hunch
something like this might happen, so I came alone.
ただ、想像以上だ。<br>……俺が来る必要、なかったんじゃないか？:
But this is beyond what I imagined... Was there even any need for me
to come?
そんなことはありません。私にだって恐怖はあります。<br>司令官様が来てくれると:
Not at all. I felt fear too. It was because I believed you would come,
Lord Commander, that I could preach God's teachings calmly.
一見、神の教えには反しているように見えますが、<br>司令官様は私の見込んだ通り:
At first glance, it might seem to contradict God's teachings, but you
are the man I believed you to be, Lord Commander.
